title: Design System: Tom Kristian Abel — Capability Radar

display.purpose: Hero heading only. Space Grotesk 700. Used exclusively for the name in the hero section.

body.purpose: All paragraphs, descriptions, long-form content. Inter 400.

mono.purpose: Data, labels, tags, code, legal text. JetBrains Mono 400.

shadows.name: surface
shadows.purpose: Card and container elevation against the background.

shadows.name: signal-glow
shadows.purpose: Cyan signal glow for active/interactive elements.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (the CSS variable --easing-out).

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Quick transitions: hover, focus, color changes.

motion.name: duration-normal
motion.purpose: Standard transitions: panel reveals, content shifts.

motion.name: radar-draw
motion.purpose: Radar chart stroke-dashoffset draw-in animation on mount.

motion.name: fade-in
motion.purpose: Content fade-in with translateY(20px) offset on scroll reveal.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Full-width cyan submit button used in the CTA contact form.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary">Saada sõnum<svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M5 12h14"/><path d="m12 5 7 7-7 7"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; width: 100%; padding: 1rem 1.5rem; background: #00D4FF; color: #020203; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.75rem; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #00D4FF; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Dark-themed text input with cyan focus border.
components.css: .ds-label { display: block; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #94A3B8; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; } .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; background: #020203; color: #F1F5F9; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; border: 1px solid #1a1a2e; border-radius: 0.75rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #64748B; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #00D4FF; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #00D4FF; }

components.name: Skill Chip
components.description: Compact cyan-tinted metadata tags on project cards.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem; background: rgba(0, 212, 255, 0.08); color: #00D4FF; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 0.5rem; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Project Card
components.description: Capability project card with subtle border and shadow.
components.html:
EchoGuard

End-to-end AI voice cloning red-teaming pipeline using RunPod Serverless GPU.

FastAPIPythonGPU
components.css: .ds-card { background: #040408; border: 1px solid #1a1a2e; border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 1.5rem; box-shadow: 0 4px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12); } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.125rem; color: #F1F5F9; margin: 0 0 0.5rem 0; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-card-body { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #94A3B8; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0 0 1rem 0; } .ds-card-chips { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.375rem; }

components.name: Navigation Link
components.description: Top nav bar link with cyan hover.
components.html: CapabilitiesResearchContactPGP
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 1rem; align-items: center; } .ds-nav-link { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #94A3B8; text-decoration: none; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); padding: 0.25rem 0; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #00D4FF; } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #00D4FF; outline-offset: 4px; }

narrative.overview: A dark instrument panel where capability appears as live radar readings. The OLED black background is the quiet room before detection; cyan traces are signals worth tracking; amber markers note points of interest. Every element emerges from darkness with purpose -- nothing glows without reason. The system rejects template-driven portfolio cliches: no cards-with-icons repeated endlessly, no hero-metric templates, no glassmorphism as default. The radar chart is not decoration -- it is navigation, table of contents, and proof, all at once. The layout is sparse but not empty; every pixel is earned.
keyCharacteristics: Dark depth, not dark mode. The black (#020203) is atmospheric, not a UI default. Surfaces emerge through subtle tonal shifts.
Three-voice typography. Space Grotesk for authority (headings), Inter for readability (body), JetBrains Mono for precision (data, labels).
Signal as structure. Cyan (#00D4FF) traces appear on interactive elements, the radar chart, and focus indicators -- never decoratively.
Radar coherence. The 6-axis radar is the information architecture. Every section maps to an axis.

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Cyan is used on <10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point -- when cyan appears, the user knows something is interactive or alive.

rules.name: The No-Decoration Rule
rules.body: No element uses cyan or amber purely for decoration. Every colored element signals something: interactivity, focus, warning, or data.

rules.name: The Beacon Rule
rules.body: Amber is reserved for legal notes, PGP encryption signals, and the amber glow in the background. It should never compete with cyan for visual priority.

rules.name: The Three-Voice Rule
rules.body: Never use one font for everything. Display always gets Space Grotesk, body always gets Inter, data/labels always get JetBrains Mono.

rules.name: The Flat First Rule
rules.body: Flat surfaces are the default. Shadows appear only to establish hierarchy on interactive or containered elements.

dos: Do use cyan (#00D4FF) as the only accent for interactive elements, focus indicators, and the radar chart.
Do use amber (#F59E0B) sparingly and deliberately -- PGP, legal notes, amber background glow.
Do use the three-typeface system: Space Grotesk for display, Inter for body, JetBrains Mono for data/labels.
Do compose the radar chart so it remains legible at 375px default viewport width.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion: disable Lenis, skip draw-in animations, instant-reveal content.
Do use tonal layering (surface-overlay tints) in preference to shadows for structural depth.
Do keep the cyan signal at <10% screen coverage. Saturation through rarity.
Do use generous rounded corners (0.75rem) on containers and inputs consistently.
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Solid colors only.
Don't use glassmorphism (backdrop-blur) as default treatment.
Don't build cards-with-icons repeated endlessly. Vary the card pattern by context.
Don't use the hero-metric template (big number + small label + stats).
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't use em dashes in copy. Use commas, colons, or periods.
Don't add shadows to every surface. Flat is the default; shadows are earned.
Don't use pure black (#000) or pure white (#fff).
Don't make the site look vibe-coded or template-driven.
